releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Health.md Visualizations 2.0.0

- Adds expanded Health.md schema v2 sample data and dashboard examples.
- Improves medication, mood/state-of-mind, workout, sleep, and activity visualizations.
- Fixes overlapping chart UI across dense/small layouts, including mood trend legends, workout trends, sleep polar, workout logs, bar-chart labels, weekday averages, sleep quality bars, activity heatmap labels, range overlays, and workout zones.
- Bundles updated `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` for Obsidian.

Validated with `npm test`.
releases.name: 2.0.0 — Health.md v2 visualizations

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Changes
- Added standalone medication section visualizations for inventory, adherence summary, per-medication dose status, adherence trend, and recent dose events.
- Added insert-wizard entries and documentation for the new medication components.

## Validation
- npm run build
- npm test
releases.name: 1.9.9 — Medication section components

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's Changed
- Add Health.md schema v2 medication parsing for counts, inventory details, and dose events.
- Add medication overview/adherence visualizations with trend and recent event table.
- Preserve schema v1/legacy compatibility and improve roll-up version handling.
- Include mood trend support and updated examples/mock health data.

## Verification
- npm test
- npm run prepare:submission
- npx tsc --noEmit
- npx eslint "src/**/*.ts"

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Adds compatibility for Health.md's first public versioned export schema (`schema_version: 1`).

- Supports `healthmd.health_data` daily exports while keeping legacy/unversioned vaults working
- Recognizes `healthmd.rollup_summary` weekly/monthly/yearly artifacts and indexes them separately from daily records
- Reads `_healthmd_data_dictionary.json` for custom exported keys, canonical keys, units, and aggregation metadata
- Handles v1 JSON metadata, Markdown/Bases frontmatter units maps, and CSV metadata rows
- Treats structured export values/units as canonical even when Markdown prose is human-readable/imperial
- Adds mixed legacy/v1 parser and data-loader coverage, including future-schema warning paths

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
Fixes the 1.9.5 community review failure by removing Obsidian 1.13-only settings API references while keeping minAppVersion at 1.12.0.

- Replaces SettingDefinitionItem/getSettingDefinitions usage with local display-only settings rendering
- Keeps frontmatter date variable support and source-note YAML fallback
- Includes rebuilt plugin assets

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
Fixes frontmatter date variable resolution for weekly journal templates when Obsidian does not provide note properties in the render context.

- Resolves {journal-start}/{journal-end} from the current note's YAML frontmatter as a fallback
- Keeps existing literal dates, datetimes, and last:N windows unchanged
- Includes rebuilt Obsidian plugin assets

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description: Sets the minimum supported Obsidian version to 1.12.0 while keeping the frontmatter date variable support and pre-1.13 settings fallback.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description: Fixes installation on Obsidian 1.12/stable by restoring the plugin minAppVersion to 1.4.10 and adding a settings-tab fallback for pre-1.13 Obsidian versions. Includes the frontmatter date variable support from 1.9.2.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Health.md Visualizations 1.9.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Bumps the minimum supported Obsidian version to 1.13.0.\n- Migrates plugin settings from the deprecated display() override to getSettingDefinitions().\n- Keeps the Obsidian review lint fixes for template handling and settings labels.
releases.name: 1.9.1 — Obsidian 1.13 settings migration

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new
- Added a Custom template option for data folder structure.
- Users can define custom folder paths with predefined variables: {year}, {month}, {week}, {day}, and {date}.
- Custom templates are sanitized and scanned to a safe derived depth.
- Existing flat, year, month, week, and day structures continue to work unchanged.

## Compatibility
- Existing vaults keep the default flat behavior unless users opt in.
- Direct files under the data folder are still loaded in nested/custom modes for gradual migrations.

## Checks
- npm test
- npm run build
- npx eslint src/**/*.ts
- npm run prepare:submission
releases.name: 1.9.0 — Custom data path templates

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new
- Added an opt-in Data folder structure setting for flat, year, month, week, and day layouts.
- Nested modes scan data subfolders while still loading files directly under the data folder for safe gradual migrations.
- File patterns can now match nested data-folder-relative paths.

## Compatibility
- Existing vaults keep the default flat behavior unless users opt in to nesting.

## Checks
- npm test
- npm run build
- npx eslint src/**/*.ts
releases.name: 1.8.0 — Nested data folders

releases.description: Health.md Visualizations 1.7.1

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Highlights

Two new \`health-viz\` block types render single-workout details from the
JSON sidecar's expanded fields (route, timeSeries, elevation, laps, splits).

- **\`workout-map\`** — Leaflet-backed GPS polyline with tile basemap, colored
  by speed (default) or heart rate (\`colorBy: hr\`). Falls back to a canvas
  polyline when tiles are disabled. Indoor sessions render a clean empty state.
- **\`workout-heart-rate\`** — per-second heart-rate line with optional Z1–Z5
  zone bands driven by a new \`maxHeartRate\` plugin setting.

## New settings

- **Maximum heart rate** — used to draw HR zone bands. Optional; bands skip
  when blank.
- **Show map tiles**, **Map tile URL**, **Map attribution** — control whether
  the workout map fetches tile imagery and from which provider. Default uses
  CARTO Voyager.

## New config keys

- \`date: YYYY-MM-DD\` and \`workout: <index>\` — pin a viz to a specific
  workout. Without them, both vizes auto-pick the most recent workout in the
  configured range.
- \`colorBy: speed | hr\` (workout-map only).

## Internal

Type extensions in \`src/types.ts\` cover \`route[]\`, \`laps[]\`, \`splits[]\`,
\`timeSeries\`, \`elevation*\`, plus the existing-but-untyped per-workout
aggregates. New \`src/workout-utils.ts\` houses unit-aware formatters that
honor the dataset's \`units\` field and prefer iOS-side pre-formatted strings.

Leaflet 1.9.4 vendored as an npm dep; its CSS appended to \`styles.css\`.
Bundle grew from ~100K to 558K — the cost of tile-based map rendering.
releases.name: 1.7.0 — Per-workout map and heart rate

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description: Release 1.6.2 for Obsidian community plugin submission.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

Eight new Apple Health–style visualizations plus a bundled `examples/apple-dashboard.md` that wires them together.

### New visualizations

| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| `summary-card` | Apple-style headline card: large KPI, sparkline, week-over-week delta. `metric` supports `heart-rate`, `steps`, `sleep-duration`, `active-calories`, `hrv`, `blood-oxygen`, `respiratory-rate`; optional `compareWindow` (`same-length`, `week`, `month`). |
| `activity-rings` | Apple's Move / Exercise / Stand concentric rings. Single-day shows one large set; multi-day renders a small-multiples grid. Tune with `moveGoal`, `exerciseGoal`, `standGoal`. |
| `heart-range` | Per-day min-to-max heart-rate capsule with an average dot. `metric`: `heart-rate` (default), `resting`, `walking`. |
| `bar-chart` | Vertical bars with the latest day highlighted. `metric`: `steps`, `activeCalories`, `exerciseMinutes`, `distance`, `sleepHours`, `flightsClimbed`. Optional `goal` draws a dashed goal line; `showAverage` toggles the mean line. |
| `sleep-schedule` | Horizontal bedtime-to-wake bars against a sunset→night→sunrise backdrop. `sleepGoal` (hours), `windowStart` / `windowEnd` (`HH:MM`). |
| `weekday-average` | Seven bars showing a metric's average by day of week with an overall-mean line. `metric`: `steps`, `activeCalories`, `exerciseMinutes`, `sleepHours`, `heartRate`, `hrv`. `weekStart`: `monday` (default) or `sunday`. |
| `trend-tile` | Compact Trends-tab card: arrow + percent delta + narrative + two-period sparkline. `metric`: `resting-heart-rate`, `hrv`, `steps`, `vo2max`, `walking-speed`, `sleep-duration`, `active-calories`. Optional `currentWindow` and `priorWindow` (days). |
| `oxygen-range` | Per-day min/max capsule + average dot for SpO₂ or respiratory rate, with a warning-zone shade. `metric`: `blood-oxygen` (default) or `respiratory-rate`. |

All eight are live-theme aware. `summary-card` and `trend-tile` render as HTML/SVG for sharper typography; the others use canvas with hover tooltips and click-to-pin.

### Bundled example

`examples/apple-dashboard.md` — a full Apple Health–style summary using all eight visualizations. Copy it into your vault to preview.

### Fixes & polish

- **bar-chart**: goal line now always renders when the goal participates in the y-axis scale. Previously a goal >110% of max would silently rescale bars to the goal while hiding the reference line.
- **Internal**: `heart-range` and `oxygen-range` share a `range-chart-core` primitive so future range-style charts drop in with a MetricSpec.

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

**Live color and theme updates** — changing a color scheme, individual color, or dark/light theme in plugin settings now instantly redraws all visible visualizations without requiring an Obsidian reload.

### How it works
Each visualization registers its `draw()` function with the plugin on mount and unregisters when the note is closed. Color and theme changes call `redrawAll()` which re-invokes every live draw in-place, resolving the current theme fresh each time.
releases.name: v1.5.1 — Reactive color & theme updates

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

**Color scheme presets** — pick a built-in palette from the plugin settings instead of tuning every color manually.

### Presets
- **Default** — original teal/amber
- **Ocean** — sky blues with navy deep sleep
- **Forest** — greens and lime
- **Sunset** — orange/pink with warm sleep tones
- **Aurora** — purple/cyan with deep indigo sleep
- **Monochrome** — slate grays throughout

### Custom override
Selecting a preset fills all 7 individual color pickers instantly. Changing any picker manually switches the dropdown to **Custom** so your tweaks are preserved.
releases.name: v1.5.0 — Color scheme presets

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

### New visualizations

| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| `hrv-trend` | Smooth line + area chart of daily Heart Rate Variability with avg/min/max stats |
| `activity-heatmap` | GitHub-style contribution grid — weeks as columns, days as rows. Supports `metric: steps` (default), `calories`, or `distance` |
| `sleep-quality-bars` | Stacked vertical bars per night showing deep, core, REM, and awake time |
| `workout-log` | Horizontal bar per workout entry, color-coded by workout type |

All new types support the same `from` / `to` / `last` date range config as existing charts, plus hover/click tooltips and stats strips.

### Example dashboards

Three ready-to-use example notes in `examples/`:

- **`daily-dashboard.md`** — Single-day deep-dive combining all relevant charts
- **`weekly-overview.md`** — Rolling 7-day view across every chart type (stays current with `last: 7`)
- **`sleep-analysis.md`** — 30-day sleep focus: stage bars, polar clock, architecture timeline, HRV, SpO2, respiratory rate

### Usage

```health-viz
type: hrv-trend
last: 30
```

```health-viz
type: activity-heatmap
last: 90
metric: steps
```

```health-viz
type: sleep-quality-bars
last: 14
height: 220
```

```health-viz
type: workout-log
last: 30
height: 240
```
releases.name: v1.4.0 — Four new chart types + example dashboards

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Bug fix

**Step spiral dots now respect the accent color setting.**

The spiral dot and line colors were computed from a hardcoded HSL hue range (always teal/green) instead of reading from the accent color in settings. Dot opacity still varies by distance to preserve the visual encoding, but the base color now follows your configured accent color.

### Charts that respond to color settings
| Chart | Accent | Secondary | Heart | Sleep colors |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Breathing Wave | ✓ | | | |
| Step Spiral | ✓ | | | |
| Vitals Rings | ✓ (steps arc) | ✓ (calories arc) | | |
| Walking Symmetry | ✓ (speed bars) | ✓→Heart gradient (asymmetry) | ✓ | |
| Sleep Architecture | | | | ✓ |
| Sleep Polar | | | | ✓ |
| Overview stats | ✓ (steps) | ✓ (distance) | ✓ (BPM) | ✓ (nights, uses REM color) |

Heart Rate Terrain and Blood Oxygen River use data-encoded HSL gradients by design and are not affected by color settings.
releases.name: v1.3.6

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

Users can now customize the colors used across all visualizations directly in plugin settings (Settings → Health.md → Colors).

### Customizable colors

- **Accent** — primary color for activity charts (steps, breathing wave, step spiral, vitals rings)
- **Secondary** — secondary color for calories arcs and walking asymmetry
- **Heart rate** — color for heart rate stats and asymmetry gradient
- **Deep sleep** — color for deep sleep stages in sleep charts
- **REM sleep** — color for REM sleep stages
- **Core sleep** — color for core sleep stages
- **Awake** — color for awake periods in sleep charts

All colors default to the original values, so existing visualizations look identical until customized.
releases.name: v1.3.5

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Fix

**sleep-polar** was rendering a blank canvas because the canvas resize at the end of the render function (used to trim excess whitespace) was clearing all drawn content. The resize is now performed *before* drawing so the canvas dimensions are correct before any painting occurs.
releases.name: v1.3.4

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's New

All visualizations now work with Markdown/YAML export format (aggregate daily data), not just JSON with timestamped samples.

### Fixes

- **heart-terrain** — falls back to a per-day average heart rate heatmap (colored gradient by min/avg/max BPM) when no intraday samples are available
- **sleep-architecture** / **sleep-polar** — synthesizes approximate sleep stages from aggregate hours (deep/REM/core/awake) + bedtime and wake time when raw stage data is absent
- **oxygen-river** — falls back to per-day blood oxygen average (with min/max bands) when no sample array is present
- **breathing-wave** — falls back to per-day respiratory rate average when no sample array is present
- **intro-stats** — "Nights Tracked" now correctly counts any night with sleep duration data, not just nights with timestamped stage data
- **markdown-parser** — fixed `walking_asymmetry_percent` key not being read from export files (was looking for `walking_asymmetry_percentage`)
releases.name: v1.3.3

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Bug Fix

- **Heart terrain blank canvas** — The chart was silently rendering nothing when using CSV or Markdown data formats. Those formats only store aggregate heart rate values (min/avg/max) and not per-sample timestamps, which the terrain heatmap requires. The chart now shows a clear message explaining that JSON format is needed, instead of an empty canvas.
releases.name: v1.3.2

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Bug fix

- **Settings now immediately refresh visualizations** — changing Data folder, File pattern, or Data format in plugin settings now re-renders all open markdown preview panes on the spot, no need to close and reopen notes.
- **Whitespace trimming** — leading/trailing spaces in the Data folder and File pattern fields are now stripped automatically, preventing invisible characters from breaking vault path lookups.
releases.name: v1.3.1

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## Custom data ranges

Every visualization can now be scoped to a custom window using three new config keys in the `health-viz` code block: `from`, `to`, and `last`. The filter is applied uniformly across all chart types.

### Date ranges

```health-viz
type: step-spiral
from: 2026-01-01
to: 2026-03-31
```

### Rolling "last N days"

```health-viz
type: heart-terrain
last: 30
```

Anchor the window on a specific day by combining `last` with `to`.

### Sub-day windows with datetimes

`from` and `to` accept ISO datetimes (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM[:SS]` with optional `Z` or `±HH:MM` timezone). On boundary days the plugin slices sample arrays (heart rate, HRV, blood oxygen, respiratory rate, sleep stages, workouts) to match the requested time window:

```health-viz
type: heart-terrain
from: 2026-04-09T06:00:00
to: 2026-04-09T12:00:00
```

### Aggregate recomputation

When boundary days are sliced, day-level fields like `averageHeartRate`, `bloodOxygenAvg`, `totalDuration`, `deepSleep`, `bedtime`, and their min/max/formatted variants are automatically recomputed from the sliced samples. Chart stats reflect the requested window instead of the full day.

A guard preserves aggregates on days that were parsed from daily summaries without per-sample data.

**Known limitation:** `activity.*` (steps, calories, exercise minutes, etc.) and `mobility.*` are exported as daily totals only, with no underlying samples. They pass through unchanged on boundary days.

### Other

- Added a project README with installation, settings, visualization types, embedding examples, and a full date range reference.
- Inline validation errors for malformed `from` / `to` / `last` values and inverted ranges.

## Installation

Copy `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into `<your vault>/.obsidian/plugins/health-md/` and reload Obsidian.
releases.name: 1.3.0 — Custom date and datetime range filtering

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's new

Charts are now interactive — hover or click on any dot, column, ring, or segment to see the underlying day/sample data in a tooltip.

### Per-visualization click targets
- **heart-terrain** — day columns → avg/min/max bpm + sample count
- **sleep-polar** — night circles → total/deep/REM/core/awake + bedtime/wake
- **step-spiral** — day dots → steps, distance, calories, exercise, flights
- **oxygen-river** — day strips → avg/min/max SpO₂ + samples
- **breathing-wave** — day segments → avg/min/max br/min + samples
- **vitals-rings** — HR dots → steps, calories, resting HR, exercise
- **walking-symmetry** — day columns → speed, asymmetry, step length, double support
- **sleep-architecture** — stage segments → start/end/duration; stripes → night summary

Click pins the tooltip; click on empty space dismisses it.
releases.name: 1.2.0 — Interactive charts

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
## What's New

- **CSV support** — Reads `Date,Category,Metric,Value,Unit` rows exported by health.md
- **Markdown support** — Parses YAML frontmatter from `.md` files with health metrics
- **Obsidian Bases support** — Reads flat YAML keys like `sleep_total_hours`, `steps`, `resting_heart_rate`
- **Configurable file pattern** — Glob filter for filenames (e.g. `*.json`, `2026-*.md`, `health-*.csv`)
- **Format auto-detection** — Detects JSON/CSV/Markdown by file extension, or force a specific format in settings
- **Deduplication** — Merges data when the same date appears across multiple files

## Settings

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Data folder | `Health` | Vault path to health data files |
| File pattern | `*` | Glob to filter filenames |
| Data format | Auto-detect | Force JSON, CSV, Markdown, or Bases |

## Install via BRAT
Add `CodyBontecou/obsidian-health-md` in BRAT settings.
releases.name: 1.1.0 — Multi-format support

releases.author: CodyBontecou
releases.description:
Initial release of Health.md Obsidian plugin.

## Features
- 9 Apple Health visualizations rendered via `health-viz` code blocks
- Reads daily JSON files from configurable vault folder
- Dark/light theme support (auto-detects Obsidian theme)
- Responsive canvas rendering with ResizeObserver
- Configurable default dimensions

## Install via BRAT
Add `CodyBontecou/obsidian-health-md` in BRAT settings.
